meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching, start shipping. Use project management frameworks, Antigravity, and Claude Code to escape tutorial hell and build revenue-generating SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a byproduct of passive consumption. To escape, you must shift from 'following along' to 'managing outcomes.' By treating your development as a series of feature-based sprints using structured project management, Antigravity for architecture, and Claude Code for execution, you force yourself into active, revenue-focused development cycles.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the code compiles, and for a moment, you feel like a developer. But beneath the surface, the hollow ache of 'tutorial hell' persists. You’ve built five To-Do lists and three weather apps, yet your SaaS dashboard remains a ghost town. You aren't building a product; you’re just curating a digital museum of other people's solutions.

Real founders don't finish tutorials—they solve problems. The shift from student to builder happens the moment you stop asking 'how do I write this function' and start asking 'how does this feature move my metrics.' It is time to stop the endless cycle of passive learning and start managing your product development with the clinical precision of a seasoned architect.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop building 'features' and define one core value proposition that solves a specific user pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Break down a [SaaS Idea] into 5 distinct, high-impact features that can be built in under 10 days. Prioritize based on speed-to-market and revenue potential.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a project roadmap file named ROADMAP.md detailing the 5 MVP features defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Model
steps.description: Before writing a single line of UI, define your database schema to ensure your data flow supports monetization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for [SaaS Idea]. Include tables for Users, Subscriptions, and Core Data. Optimize for scalability and Stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Generate prisma schema file based on the Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Initialize the Tech Stack
steps.description: Standardize your environment to avoid configuration fatigue. Use Claude Code to scaffold the project structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a high-performance, production-ready directory structure for a Next.js/Supabase SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Initialize project with the recommended folder structure and install necessary dependencies for authentication and database ORM.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build one feature at a time. Never move to the next until the current feature is unit-tested and functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write clean, modular code for the [Specific Feature] component. Ensure it follows SOLID principles and includes error handling for production environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Implement the [Specific Feature] logic with robust error handling and type safety.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Get your MVP in front of real users immediately. Feedback beats perfection every time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a production SaaS application, covering CI/CD pipelines, environment variables, and monitoring integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a deployment script and a Vercel-ready configuration file for this project.'

pro_tips: Treat your codebase as a liability, not an asset. If a feature doesn't directly contribute to user retention or revenue, delete it.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests first; it forces the AI to understand the logic before it generates the implementation.
Never spend more than 4 hours on a single bug. If you're stuck, use Antigravity to pivot the architecture rather than patching bad code.
Document your 'why' in the code comments, not just the 'how.' Future-you will thank you when you need to refactor for scale.
Automate your feedback loop. Integrate a simple 'Report a Bug' or 'Request Feature' form in your MVP to capture user intent early.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, spend 80% of your time on distribution and user interviews. Use the feedback to inform your next sprint cycle.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the methodology to stop being a student and start being a founder. The market doesn't care about your code; it cares about your solution. Stop over-engineering and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
